summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on September 18, 2023. The board approved a fence variance (BZA case 2023-010) allowing a 10-foot chain link fence with slats at 6722 State Route 132, which is a B2 commercial property requiring the higher fence per Ohio Board of Pharmacy security requirements for a proposed pharmacy use. The meeting also briefly referenced BZA cases 2022-011, 012, and 013. The next scheduled meeting was set for October 16th.
decisions: Approved BZA case 2023-010: 10-foot fence variance at 6722 State Route 132 (2-foot variance from 8-foot maximum)
action_items: Applicant to coordinate with Alyssa (zoning staff) regarding the updated/smaller fenced-in area dimensions
Staff to follow up on list of boards and figure out remaining details related to the approved variance
key_amounts: 10-foot fence requested (2-foot variance from 8-foot maximum allowed in B2 commercial district)
Fenced area reduced by 10 feet closer to the building from original plan
notable_topics: BZA fence variance request for 10-foot security fence at commercial property
Ohio Board of Pharmacy security requirements for proposed pharmacy use
notes: This is a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting, not a regular Board of Trustees meeting, despite repeated transcript references to 'Board of Trustees Meeting.' The transcript contains significant garbled/repeated text artifacts likely from automated transcription. The meeting appeared brief, running from approximately 6:56 PM to 6:58 PM for the reopened session. The applicant stated they originally applied for an 8-foot fence but were required by the Ohio Board of Pharmacy to increase it to 10 feet or build a garage structure instead.
title: BZA 9-18-2023
